input.title: Dynamic Arrays with std::vector

output.technical: `std::vector` is a sequence container that encapsulates dynamic size arrays. It manages its own heap memory, automatically reallocating and moving elements when its capacity is exceeded.
output.beginner: A vector is a super-powered list. Unlike basic arrays where you have to know the size in advance, a vector can grow and shrink automatically as you add or remove items.
output.analogy: A regular array is like a bus; it has a fixed number of seats. A vector is like a train; if it gets full, the conductor just attaches another car to the back.
output.solution: Use `std::vector` as your default array in C++. It is safe, fast, and handles memory automatically.
output.prevention: Pass vectors by `const &` to avoid expensive copies.

output.technical: `std::vector` is a sequence container that encapsulates dynamic size arrays. It manages its own heap memory, automatically reallocating and moving elements when its capacity is exceeded.
output.beginner: A vector is a super-powered list. Unlike basic arrays where you have to know the size in advance, a vector can grow and shrink automatically as you add or remove items.
output.analogy: A regular array is like a bus; it has a fixed number of seats. A vector is like a train; if it gets full, the conductor just attaches another car to the back.
output.solution: Use `std::vector` as your default array in C++. It is safe, fast, and handles memory automatically.
output.prevention: Pass vectors by `const &` to avoid expensive copies.
